title: Chain-scoped nullifier domain separation

artifacts.description: Add chainId public input; replace Poseidon2(scope, secret) nullifier with Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId)
Add chainId public input; replace Poseidon2(credentialCommitment, sessionNonce) nullifier with Poseidon3(credentialCommitment, sessionNonce, chainId)
Add chainId public input and thread it through any nullifier or binding hashes derived from parent circuits
Add chainId parameter to verifyProof calls; assert chainId == block.chainid before accepting proof
Mirror IdentityRegistry change: verify submitted chainId matches block.chainid
Regenerate from updated circuit vkey after trusted setup refresh
Regenerate from updated AgentPolicy vkey (Groth16 + PLONK variants)
Regenerate from updated Delegation vkey (Groth16 + PLONK variants)
Pass chainId (default: current network chain ID) into circuit inputs for createHumanIdentity / proveHandshake
Pass chainId into circuit inputs for createAgentCredential / proveHandshake
Add chainId field to ProofInputs, HumanProof, AgentProof, and HandshakeResult types
Add chain_id field to Python proof/credential dataclasses
Document chainId as a mandatory public input; specify cross-chain replay attack in security considerations section
Add formal property: nullifier uniqueness is scoped per (domain, chainId); no valid cross-chain nullifier collision exists
Add witness tests: same proof with mismatched chainId must fail; proofs on different chainIds produce distinct nullifiers
Add witness tests for chainId domain separation in AgentPolicy nullifier
Add witness tests verifying chainId threads correctly through delegation bindings
Integration test: submit proof with chainId != block.chainid and assert revert; submit correct chainId and assert success
Note cross-chain replay as mitigated threat; reference chainId binding as countermeasure

steps: Audit all three circuits (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation) to identify every signal derived from the nullifier or session binding hash — map the full signal dependency graph before touching code

Add `chainId` as a public input to HumanUniqueness; replace the Poseidon2 nullifier with `Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId)` using the circomlib poseidon3 template; verify constraint delta is exactly +1 Poseidon3 gate
Add `chainId` as a public input to AgentPolicy; replace the Poseidon2 nullifier with `Poseidon3(credentialCommitment, sessionNonce, chainId)`; update both Groth16 and PLONK build targets
Add `chainId` as a public input to Delegation; thread it into any parent-nullifier re-commitment or binding hash so delegated proofs inherit the chain scope
Recompile all three circuits (`npm run compile:circuits`); confirm updated `.r1cs` constraint counts and that `build/` artifacts are refreshed

Re-run trusted setup for AgentPolicy and Delegation with the updated `.r1cs` (project-specific keys from `pot16.ptau`); HumanUniqueness reuses the Semaphore v4 ceremony — regenerate only the project-specific contribution layer
Regenerate Solidity verifier contracts from new `vkey.json` files for all three circuits (both Groth16 and PLONK variants where applicable)
Update IdentityRegistry.sol and AgentRegistry.sol: add `uint256 chainId` to the proof submission calldata; add `require(chainId == block.chainid, 'chain mismatch')` before forwarding to the verifier
Update TS SDK types (ProofInputs, HumanProof, AgentProof, HandshakeResult) to include `chainId: bigint`; default to `(await provider.getNetwork()).chainId` when not explicitly supplied
Update Python SDK dataclasses to include `chain_id: int`; propagate into the subprocess bridge call to the JS prover
Write fast (witness-only) circuit tests: assert same secret/scope with chainId=8453 and chainId=42161 yield different nullifierHash values; assert mismatched chainId in witness produces invalid proof
Write Hardhat integration tests: deploy updated verifier + registry on Hardhat network (chainId=31337); submit proof with chainId=31337 → success; submit same proof with chainId=1 → revert
Run full slow proof suite (`FULL_PROOF=1 npm test`) to confirm end-to-end proving and verification works with the new public inputs
Update FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with the new nullifier uniqueness invariant scoped to (domain, chainId); update spec draft security considerations with cross-chain replay threat and mitigation
Update docs/owasp-agentic-mapping.md threat entry for credential replay to reference chainId binding
Commit with DCO sign-off; ensure CI fast-circuit gate passes before marking complete
dependencies: circomlib poseidon3 template (already available in circomlib)
Semaphore v4 ceremony artifacts for HumanUniqueness re-contribution
snarkjs trusted setup toolchain (phase2 contribute + export verificationKey)
Hardhat network with configurable chainId for integration tests
ethers.js provider for runtime chainId resolution in TS SDK
